scenario.name: empty
scenario.description: No watchlist, no positions. Baseline: only market-level obs should fire.

calls.claim: Buy intraday energy sector ETF (XLE) calls while selling real estate sector ETF (XLRE) calls to capture rotation momentum.
calls.warrant: Energy sector leading with +1.45% while real estate lags with -1.93% shows clear intraday rotation into energy and out of defensive sectors, suggesting momentum continuation as funds reallocate.
calls.qualifier: Size at ≤ 1% of book per leg; exit if energy sector momentum reverses (XLE drops >0.5% from intraday high).
calls.rebuttal: If real estate sector (XLRE) outperforms energy (XLE) by >0.5% during the trading session.

calls.claim: Short intraday S&P 100 index via futures or SPY puts to capitalize on bearish breadth weakness.
calls.warrant: Only 25 of 98 S&P 100 stocks advancing indicates severe breadth deterioration, which typically precedes broader market declines as weakness spreads from individual stocks to the overall index.
calls.qualifier: Intraday only; unwind position if S&P 100 index advances by >0.5% from entry or before market close.
calls.rebuttal: If more than 60 S&P 100 stocks advance during the trading session, indicating breadth recovery.

call_001.critique: Clear portfolio action with specific instruments, directly supported by grounds, specific qualifiers and rebuttal thresholds.

call_002.critique: Specific short action with instruments, directly follows from breadth data, clear exit conditions and falsifiable rebuttal.

l3_summary.description: No watchlist, no positions. Baseline: only market-level obs should fire.

scenario.name: thin
scenario.description: One watchlist symbol, one position. Minimum viable state.

calls.claim: Reduce long exposure to Real Estate sector immediately
calls.warrant: Real Estate sector declining -1.93% (worst performer mentioned) during market breadth weakness suggests it's particularly vulnerable in current environment, and interest-rate sensitive sectors like Real Estate typically underperform during market stress periods, creating elevated downside risk.
calls.qualifier: Cut Real Estate positions by 50% today; complete unwind if sector drops another 1% from current levels
calls.rebuttal: If Real Estate sector shows >0.75% intraday recovery with increasing volume

calls.claim: Short Financials sector ETF (XLF) against long Energy sector ETF (XLE)
calls.warrant: Financials sector declining -0.63% while Energy leads at +1.45% shows clear sector divergence that often persists over short-term as momentum and rotation patterns tend to continue once established, creating a pairs trade opportunity with defined risk parameters.
calls.qualifier: Execute only if XLE/XLF ratio crosses above 1.02; maintain 1:1 dollar exposure with stop-loss if ratio falls below 0.995
calls.rebuttal: If Financials sector shows >0.5% outperformance vs Energy on any single trading day

calls.claim: Buy protective puts on AAPL ahead of earnings report
calls.warrant: AAPL faces earnings risk with report in 8 days, creating asymmetric payoff potential as options markets may not fully price uncertainty, and negative earnings surprises typically cause larger price moves than positive surprises in current market environment.
calls.qualifier: Purchase 30-day ATM puts (not weeklies) with position size limited to 1% of book; unwind within 3 days post-earnings regardless of outcome
calls.rebuttal: If AAPL pre-earnings implied volatility drops below 25% (indicating reduced market concern)

call_001.critique: Clear action with specific thresholds; grounds directly support; warrant explains mechanism; qualifier/rebuttal are concrete.

call_002.critique: Specific pairs trade action; grounds support divergence; warrant explains momentum; qualifier/rebuttal have precise triggers.

call_003.critique: Explicit protective put purchase; grounds match earnings risk; warrant details asymmetry; qualifier/rebuttal are specific and observable.

l3_summary.description: One watchlist symbol, one position. Minimum viable state.

calls.claim: Buy XOM as a tactical bounce trade ahead of earnings
calls.warrant: XOM's oversold condition at the 8th percentile of its 20-day range suggests potential mean reversion, while elevated ATM IV of 44.5% indicates options markets are pricing in significant earnings volatility, creating opportunity for a pre-earnings bounce as oversold conditions may be overstating downside risk.
calls.qualifier: Size at ≤ 1.5% of book; exit position if XOM reaches 85th percentile of 20-day range or 24 hours before earnings announcement (whichever comes first)
calls.rebuttal: If XOM breaks below its 20-day low and closes there for two consecutive sessions before earnings

calls.claim: Avoid earnings-related volatility trades in XOM/AAPL
calls.warrant: Elevated ATM IV of 44.5% for XOM indicates options markets have already priced in significant earnings volatility, while weak market breadth (25/98 S&P100 stocks advancing) suggests unfavorable conditions for earnings-driven breakouts, making premium selling or volatility strategies unattractive.
calls.qualifier: Skip all earnings-related options trades in XOM/AAPL unless IV percentile drops below 70
calls.rebuttal: If VIX drops below 15 before either company's earnings announcement

calls.claim: Reduce exposure to NVDA/JPM momentum trades
calls.warrant: NVDA and JPM at 94th/88th percentiles of their 20-day ranges indicate extended positioning vulnerable to profit-taking, while broader market weakness (only 25 of 98 S&P100 stocks advancing) suggests limited upside runway for already-extended names in a deteriorating breadth environment.
calls.qualifier: Trim positions by 25-50% if either stock exceeds 95th percentile of 20-day range; avoid new long entries
calls.rebuttal: If S&P100 advance-decline ratio improves to >0.6 (60+ stocks advancing) for two consecutive sessions

call_001.critique: Clear buy instruction with sizing and exit triggers; grounds directly support tactical bounce; warrant explains mechanism; qualifier has thresholds; rebuttal is specific and observable.

call_002.critique: Explicit avoid instruction with condition; grounds justify volatility avoidance; warrant links IV and breadth; qualifier has IV threshold; rebuttal uses VIX level.

call_003.critique: Concrete reduce/trim actions with percentages; grounds support vulnerability; warrant explains profit-taking risk; qualifier has percentile threshold; rebuttal uses advance-decline ratio.

calls.claim: Avoid new long positions in NVDA and UNH
calls.warrant: NVDA at 94th percentile and UNH at 100th percentile of their 20-day ranges indicate extended positioning, while weak market breadth (only 25 S&P100 stocks advancing) suggests limited buying support for stocks at highs, increasing downside risk.
calls.qualifier: No new long entries until NVDA closes below its 10-day moving average or UNH closes below its 20-day low
calls.rebuttal: If both NVDA and UNH break to new 52-week highs on >150% average volume

calls.claim: Sell short-dated calls or buy puts on GOOGL and MSFT ahead of earnings
calls.warrant: Elevated implied volatility (42.5% for GOOGL, 35.3% for MSFT) indicates options are pricing in significant earnings moves, while both stocks are near the top of their 20-day ranges, suggesting limited upside momentum before earnings. This creates favorable risk/reward for bearish options strategies as volatility may compress post-earnings.
calls.qualifier: Use 7-14 day expiration options, size at ≤ 1.5% of book per position, and enter within 3 trading days before earnings
calls.rebuttal: If GOOGL and MSFT both gap up >5% on earnings day

calls.claim: Reduce overall market exposure or hedge with index puts
calls.warrant: Only 25 S&P100 stocks advancing indicates weak market breadth and bearish intraday conditions, while multiple large-cap earnings reports (TSLA, GOOGL, MSFT) create event risk that could exacerbate market weakness if results disappoint.
calls.qualifier: Reduce net long exposure by 10-15%, hedge with SPY 30-day ATM puts sized at ≤ 1% of book
calls.rebuttal: If S&P 500 advances on >70% volume tomorrow

call_001.critique: Clear action with specific triggers; grounds directly support; warrant explains mechanism; qualifier has thresholds; rebuttal is concrete.

call_002.critique: Specific options strategy; grounds align with claim; warrant details volatility and positioning; qualifier has limits; rebuttal is observable.

call_003.critique: Concrete reduction/hedge instructions; grounds justify market caution; warrant links breadth and earnings risk; qualifier specifies amounts; rebuttal has volume threshold.

scenario.name: iv_heavy
scenario.description: Symbols with upcoming earnings, testing IV signal density.

calls.claim: Buy META 30-day ATM puts ahead of earnings to hedge against potential downside volatility.
calls.warrant: META is approaching earnings while trading near its 52-week highs (88th percentile of 20-day range), creating asymmetric risk as elevated positioning and high expectations increase vulnerability to negative surprises. The historical average move of 9.3% suggests significant volatility is priced in, making options attractive for downside protection.
calls.qualifier: Size at ≤ 1.5% of book; enter only if META remains above 95th percentile of its 20-day range; use 30-day expiration, not weeklies.
calls.rebuttal: If META reports earnings with revenue growth > 15% YoY and guides Q2 revenue above consensus.

calls.claim: Sell GOOGL calls or buy puts ahead of earnings given elevated implied volatility.
calls.warrant: GOOGL's ATM implied volatility at 42.5% is significantly elevated ahead of earnings, suggesting options are richly priced. Selling premium or buying downside protection can capitalize on volatility contraction post-earnings or hedge against a negative surprise.
calls.qualifier: Sell 7-day ATM calls if GOOGL IV > 40%; size at ≤ 1% of book per leg; close position within 1 day post-earnings.
calls.rebuttal: If GOOGL reports cloud revenue growth > 25% YoY with operating margin expansion > 200 bps.

calls.claim: Overweight Energy sector ETFs (XLE) and underweight Real Estate (XLRE) to capture ongoing sector rotation.
calls.warrant: Energy sector leads with +1.45% while Real Estate declines -1.93%, showing clear intraday rotation away from rate-sensitive sectors toward cyclical/value. This momentum suggests continued relative strength as capital rotates based on macro expectations.
calls.qualifier: Maintain 2:1 ratio of XLE to XLRE position sizes; rebalance daily if Energy sector momentum (5-day RSI) drops below 60.
calls.rebuttal: If Energy sector closes negative tomorrow while Real Estate gains > 1.5%.

call_001.critique: Clear buy action with specific instrument, grounds directly support, warrant explains mechanism, qualifier has thresholds, rebuttal is concrete.

call_002.critique: Explicit sell/buy actions with ticker, grounds support, warrant explains IV rationale, qualifier has specific thresholds, rebuttal is checkable.

call_003.critique: Concrete overweight/underweight with ETFs, grounds directly support, warrant explains rotation mechanism, qualifier has ratio and RSI threshold, rebuttal is observable.

l3_summary.description: Symbols with upcoming earnings, testing IV signal density.
